title: Environmental Quality Commission Approves Delay to Clean Fuels and Climate Protection Program Deadlines
content:
The Environmental Quality Commission gave its approval Friday for the Oregon Department of Environmental Quality to grant a variance from the normal April 30 compliance deadline for the Clean Fuels Program, delaying it for program participants until Friday, May 30.

The reporting and compliance system that the CFP uses, the Oregon Fuels Report System, was taken down in response to the cyberattack that affected DEQ on April 9. It is not yet back up and running, but DEQ hopes to fix that soon. Given those issues, DEQ is delaying the CFPâ€™s Annual Compliance Report deadline to Friday, May 30, 2025. That new deadline gives time for regulated parties and DEQ staff to work through a number of needed corrections to the data, recalculate their credit balances, and allow regulated parties time to purchase any credits they may need for compliance after they correct their 2024 data. Since the Greenhouse Gas Reporting Programâ€™s annual report for liquid fuel suppliers is submitted through the same reporting system, incorporates CFP quarterly data, and is normally due on April 30, DEQ is also extending that programâ€™s deadline to Friday, May 30. This preserves the alignment between the two programs. Additionally, Greenhouse Gas Reporting Program data is critical to the effective implementation of the Climate Protection Program. The reporting programâ€™s data is used to determine compliance obligations for covered entities and used to determine the number of compliance instruments DEQ distributes to each covered entity each year. DEQ had intended to distribute the 2025 compliance instruments to covered fuels suppliers no later than June 30, 2025. With the deadline extension, it is critical that reporting entities, particularly those regulated by the Climate Protection Program, have an opportunity to make corrections to reported emissions data prior to the 2025 distribution of compliance instruments. DEQ now intends to distribute 2025 compliance instruments no later than July 30, 2025. DEQ is not changing the methodology used to calculate the number of 2025 compliance instruments each covered fuel supplier will receive, only the timeline for distribution.
author: Mansift Kaur
